description: Free-tier user upgrades to Standard via Razorpay checkout — full payment-to-tier-promotion cycle

steps.description: PRE-CONDITION CHECK: This journey requires Razorpay test keys configured in the jobportal backend (QA_RAZORPAY_KEY_ID + QA_RAZORPAY_KEY_SECRET in /opt/jobportal/.env on EC2). If the checkout page fails to load, record 'Razorpay test keys not configured' and STOP — this is an infrastructure gap, not a product defect. Navigate to http://localhost:3002/auth/login and log in with qa-jp-free@phronex.com / JHTest2026#Free! — this account holds a FREE tier JP grant. Wait for the dashboard to load.
Navigate to http://localhost:3002/jp/subscription. Verify the page loads and shows the current tier as 'Free Seeker' (or equivalent). Note the current tier label exactly.

Find and click the upgrade button for the Standard tier plan. A Razorpay checkout modal or redirect should occur. If no upgrade button is visible, or if the button is disabled, FAIL with 'Upgrade CTA missing or disabled for free-tier user'.

In the Razorpay test checkout, fill in test card details: Card number 4111 1111 1111 1111, Expiry 12/28, CVV 123, Name 'QA Test User'. If asked for OTP or 3D-Secure, use the test success code (usually '1234' or click 'Success' in the Razorpay test overlay). Complete the payment.

After payment: Razorpay should redirect back to the portal. Wait up to 15 seconds for the redirect to complete and the page to settle. Navigate to http://localhost:3002/jp/subscription. What tier label is now shown? It MUST read 'Standard Seeker'. FAIL if still 'Free Seeker' — this means the webhook did not fire or tier promotion failed.

Verify post-upgrade state: (1) No upgrade CTA pointing to Standard is visible (user is already Standard); (2) An upgrade CTA to Pro may be present — that is correct; (3) Check browser console for any JS errors during the upgrade flow. Report: what happened at each step, was tier promoted correctly, any errors seen. Screenshot the subscription page after upgrade showing the new tier label.

description: Verify tier is correctly reflected in the JP dashboard and API after Razorpay webhook fires — catches stale-cache and immediate-reflection failures

steps.description: PRE-CONDITION: Run jp-pay-01 first (this journey assumes the qa-jp-free@phronex.com account was just upgraded to Standard). Login as qa-jp-free@phronex.com / JHTest2026#Free! and wait for dashboard.
Navigate to http://localhost:3002/jp/dashboard. Is the header, sidebar, or user profile section showing the correct tier? It should reflect Standard, not Free.

Navigate to http://localhost:3002/jp/subscription. Confirm tier label is 'Standard Seeker'. Check: does the feature limit table (portal count, scan frequency, etc.) show Standard-tier limits rather than Free-tier limits?

Navigate to http://localhost:3002/jp/portals. Try adding a second portal (the Free tier allows only 1 portal; Standard allows more). Click '+ Add Portal', fill name='qa-standard-test-portal', keywords='engineer'. Does the form allow submission, or is it blocked with a 'Free tier limit reached' error? If submission is blocked: FAIL — tier promotion did not propagate to portal feature gates.
If the portal was created in step 4: delete it (click delete on 'qa-standard-test-portal', confirm removal) to clean up test data.

Check browser console for errors. Report: (1) tier label in dashboard vs subscription page consistent? (2) Feature gate for portal add reflects Standard limits? (3) Any 403 or tier-check errors in network tab? Screenshot the subscription page showing Standard tier confirmed.
